headline: Innovation Post tenant slate firms up — four named tenants
paragraphs: The Innovation Post — the centerpiece commercial space inside the $53M RAISE footprint — will initially house four named tenants : Wurtec Incorporated, WorkSpring, Junior Achievement of Northwestern Ohio, and SEGULA Technologies USA. Together they span industrial supply (Wurtec), flexible office (WorkSpring), workforce/education (Junior Achievement), and engineering services (SEGULA — a French-headquartered global engineering firm). City planners separately note that ~67% of Uptown is currently parking lots and green space — the basis for the "extraordinary opportunity for new development" framing during the RAISE design phase.
why_it_matters: First time the Uptown redevelopment story has named-tenant specificity rather than infrastructure-and-vision rhetoric. Four tenants is enough to start sketching second-order effects — lunch/service demand, daytime population shift, parking absorption. SEGULA Technologies in particular signals to other engineering and advanced-manufacturing companies that Uptown is viable, which compounds. The 67% parking/green-space stat is the political framing developers should expect through 2026 design-phase community meetings.
search_terms: Innovation Post Toledo Uptown Wurtec WorkSpring Junior Achievement SEGULA Technologies tenants
